articles.title: Selective investing the way forward as AI, valuations reshape market trends: Dipan Mehta
articles.content: Dipan Mehta advises caution on large-cap IT due to AI disruption, favoring midcap players like Persistent Systems and Tata Elxsi. While Trent shows momentum, high valuations warrant tempered expectations. Seasonal plays like cooling solutions, exemplified by Symphony, present a potential opportunity amid a hot summer.
articles.excerpt: Dipan Mehta advises caution on large-cap IT due to AI disruption, favoring midcap players like Persistent Systems and Tata Elxsi. While Trent shows momentum, high valuations warrant tempered expectations. Seasonal plays like cooling solutions, exemplified by Symphony, present a potential opportunity amid a hot summer.

articles.title: Mega conviction! 10 stocks where FIIs raised over 2% stake when everything in markets was crashing
articles.content: Despite massive FII outflows amid global volatility, foreign investors sharply increased stakes in 10 Indian stocks during Q4. Retail, financials, manufacturing, and specialty chemicals saw the strongest gains, signalling selective high-conviction buying focused on companies with strong earnings visibility, sector tailwinds, and resilient domestic demand.
articles.excerpt: Despite massive FII outflows amid global volatility, foreign investors sharply increased stakes in 10 Indian stocks during Q4. Retail, financials, manufacturing, and specialty chemicals saw the strongest gains, signalling selective high-conviction buying focused on companies with strong earnings visibility, sector tailwinds, and resilient domestic demand.

articles.title: Power Systems shares jumps 7% after Motilal Oswal initiates ‘Buy’ with Rs 340 target
articles.content: Fujiyama Power Systems shares surged over 6% after Motilal Oswal initiated coverage with a Buy rating and Rs 340 target price. Strong solar market prospects, capacity expansion, and policy support boosted sentiment, although technical indicators signal overbought conditions, suggesting potential near-term consolidation despite a firmly positive broader trend outlook.
articles.excerpt: Fujiyama Power Systems shares surged over 6% after Motilal Oswal initiated coverage with a Buy rating and Rs 340 target price. Strong solar market prospects, capacity expansion, and policy support boosted sentiment, although technical indicators signal overbought conditions, suggesting potential near-term consolidation despite a firmly positive broader trend outlook.

articles.title: Vijay Kedia effect: This smallcap solar stock rallies 21% in two days. Do you own?
articles.content: Websol Energy System shares surged 21% in two sessions after investor Vijay Kedia emerged as a key shareholder. The rally was driven by improved sentiment and strong price momentum. The company also announced a board meeting to consider Q4 results and a potential dividend, its first in nearly 17 years.
articles.excerpt: Websol Energy System shares surged 21% in two sessions after investor Vijay Kedia emerged as a key shareholder. The rally was driven by improved sentiment and strong price momentum. The company also announced a board meeting to consider Q4 results and a potential dividend, its first in nearly 17 years.

articles.title: US Stock Market: IBM shares slide after software weakness clouds Q1 results
articles.content: IBM shares fell in after-hours trading after its Q1 results signalled a slowdown in revenue growth, particularly in its software division. While earnings beat estimates, concerns around AI-driven disruption and softer segment performance weighed on sentiment. Strong infrastructure growth and enterprise demand provided some support to the overall outlook.
articles.excerpt: IBM shares fell in after-hours trading after its Q1 results signalled a slowdown in revenue growth, particularly in its software division. While earnings beat estimates, concerns around AI-driven disruption and softer segment performance weighed on sentiment. Strong infrastructure growth and enterprise demand provided some support to the overall outlook.

articles.title: Delta Corp shares slide over 3% as Q4 profit plunges 90% to Rs 16.45 crore
articles.content: Delta Corp shares fell over 3% after reporting a 90% year-on-year drop in Q4 profit, alongside weaker revenue and EBITDA performance. Despite announcing a dividend, the stock remains under pressure, with long-term weakness intact even as valuations appear inexpensive and technical indicators signal mixed momentum with overbought near-term conditions.
articles.excerpt: Delta Corp shares fell over 3% after reporting a 90% year-on-year drop in Q4 profit, alongside weaker revenue and EBITDA performance. Despite announcing a dividend, the stock remains under pressure, with long-term weakness intact even as valuations appear inexpensive and technical indicators signal mixed momentum with overbought near-term conditions.

articles.title: Havells shares tank 6% post weak Q4 show. Morgan Stanley cuts to underweight, Goldman Sachs trims target
articles.content: Havells shares fell sharply on Thursday after a weak Q4, with revenue growth slowing and EBITDA declining. A sharp rise in other income boosted profit, but brokerages flagged margin pressures and muted demand. Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock, while Goldman Sachs and Nuvama trimmed targets but maintained positive long-term views.
articles.excerpt: Havells shares fell sharply on Thursday after a weak Q4, with revenue growth slowing and EBITDA declining. A sharp rise in other income boosted profit, but brokerages flagged margin pressures and muted demand. Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock, while Goldman Sachs and Nuvama trimmed targets but maintained positive long-term views.

articles.title: HSBC downgrades Indian stocks to ‘underweight’ as oil shock clouds earnings recovery
articles.content: HSBC has downgraded Indian equities to "underweight," citing surging energy prices from the Middle East war as a threat to the country's earnings recovery. With Brent crude above $100 a barrel, inflation and growth risks are elevated, making India less attractive than North East Asian peers. Earnings forecasts are expected to be revised lower, and foreign investor concerns are mounting.
articles.excerpt: HSBC has downgraded Indian equities to "underweight," citing surging energy prices from the Middle East war as a threat to the country's earnings recovery. With Brent crude above $100 a barrel, inflation and growth risks are elevated, making India less attractive than North East Asian peers. Earnings forecasts are expected to be revised lower, and foreign investor concerns are mounting.

articles.title: 10 smallcap stocks jump up to 99% in April alone! Do you own any?
Tech Mahindra deal wins & AI push offer hope for FY27; management guidance key trigger: Aditya Shah
articles.content: Tech Mahindra reported steady quarterly results. A significant deal with Orange in Europe offers future revenue potential. The company is supporting AI firms through cloud services. A recent headcount reduction is noted. Analysts are awaiting management guidance for FY27 to assess future growth prospects. Dividend yield is a key attraction for investors.
articles.excerpt: Tech Mahindra reported steady quarterly results. A significant deal with Orange in Europe offers future revenue potential. The company is supporting AI firms through cloud services. A recent headcount reduction is noted. Analysts are awaiting management guidance for FY27 to assess future growth prospects. Dividend yield is a key attraction for investors.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Bessent signals expansion of currency swap lines to Gulf and Asian nations
articles.content: Several Gulf allies and Asian economies are seeking US dollar liquidity through foreign exchange swap lines, according to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. This move aims to stabilize global financial markets amid geopolitical stress and currency volatility, reinforcing the US dollar's dominant global role. Discussions are ongoing, particularly with the UAE.
articles.excerpt: Several Gulf allies and Asian economies are seeking US dollar liquidity through foreign exchange swap lines, according to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. This move aims to stabilize global financial markets amid geopolitical stress and currency volatility, reinforcing the US dollar's dominant global role. Discussions are ongoing, particularly with the UAE.

articles.title: Global Market: What drove the Nikkei past the 60,000 mark today
articles.content: Japan’s Nikkei surged past the 60,000 mark for the first time, driven by strong gains in technology stocks and easing geopolitical tensions. However, the index later pared gains as broader market weakness and narrow participation weighed on sentiment, highlighting a rally concentrated in select large-cap tech names rather than broad-based strength.
articles.excerpt: Japan’s Nikkei surged past the 60,000 mark for the first time, driven by strong gains in technology stocks and easing geopolitical tensions. However, the index later pared gains as broader market weakness and narrow participation weighed on sentiment, highlighting a rally concentrated in select large-cap tech names rather than broad-based strength.

articles.title: Wall Street at record highs! Why US stocks remain a winning bet
articles.content: US equities are defying global market headwinds, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting record highs. This rally is driven by strong corporate earnings and supported by historical market momentum. For Indian investors, currency depreciation has amplified returns, reinforcing the case for global diversification.
articles.excerpt: US equities are defying global market headwinds, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting record highs. This rally is driven by strong corporate earnings and supported by historical market momentum. For Indian investors, currency depreciation has amplified returns, reinforcing the case for global diversification.

articles.title: Tech Mahindra shares fall nearly 2% after Q4. Goldman Sachs, Motilal Oswal, other brokerages weigh in
articles.content: Tech Mahindra shares slipped after reporting a 16% rise in quarterly profit, with revenue growth supporting sentiment despite cautious outlooks. The stock remained under pressure as brokerages flagged valuation concerns, even as strong deal wins and AI-led momentum provided some support, keeping short-term price action mixed amid uneven market response.
articles.excerpt: Tech Mahindra shares slipped after reporting a 16% rise in quarterly profit, with revenue growth supporting sentiment despite cautious outlooks. The stock remained under pressure as brokerages flagged valuation concerns, even as strong deal wins and AI-led momentum provided some support, keeping short-term price action mixed amid uneven market response.

articles.title: Trent shares slip after Q4 results. What are Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, other top brokerages saying?
articles.content: Trent Ltd shares slipped despite strong Q4 results, where net profit rose 26% year-on-year to Rs 400 crore. Revenue also grew 19% to Rs 5,028 crore, reflecting solid operational performance even as the stock saw mild profit booking.
articles.excerpt: Trent Ltd shares slipped despite strong Q4 results, where net profit rose 26% year-on-year to Rs 400 crore. Revenue also grew 19% to Rs 5,028 crore, reflecting solid operational performance even as the stock saw mild profit booking.

articles.title: Why is stock market falling today? Sensex plunges 450 pts, Nifty breaks 24,300. 6 reasons behind the selloff
articles.content: Indian equity benchmarks declined for a second straight session, pressured by a spike in crude oil prices, a weakening rupee and rising geopolitical tensions. Broad-based selling, FII outflows and rising bond yields weighed on sentiment, while volatility increased. Analysts expect near-term market direction to remain uncertain amid global risks and elevated oil prices.
articles.excerpt: Indian equity benchmarks declined for a second straight session, pressured by a spike in crude oil prices, a weakening rupee and rising geopolitical tensions. Broad-based selling, FII outflows and rising bond yields weighed on sentiment, while volatility increased. Analysts expect near-term market direction to remain uncertain amid global risks and elevated oil prices.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Prices Today: Silver tumbles Rs 6,100, gold dips Rs 1,000 as rising crude, little progress in Iran peace talks dent mood. Time to sell?
articles.content: Gold and silver prices fell sharply on MCX amid rising crude oil prices and uncertainty around US-Iran peace talks, raising concerns over prolonged high interest rates. Analysts expect continued volatility, advising investors to book profits and wait for dips, while tracking global cues like dollar movement and inflation trends.
articles.excerpt: Gold and silver prices fell sharply on MCX amid rising crude oil prices and uncertainty around US-Iran peace talks, raising concerns over prolonged high interest rates. Analysts expect continued volatility, advising investors to book profits and wait for dips, while tracking global cues like dollar movement and inflation trends.

articles.title: Infosys shares fall 1% ahead of Q4 earnings; IT major teams up with OpenAI
articles.content: Infosys shares dipped as the IT giant prepares to announce its Q4 FY26 earnings. The company also revealed a significant partnership with OpenAI, integrating its AI tools like Codex into Infosys' Topaz model. This collaboration aims to revolutionize software development and modernization for enterprises, moving beyond experimentation to practical, scaled AI deployment.
articles.excerpt: Infosys shares dipped as the IT giant prepares to announce its Q4 FY26 earnings. The company also revealed a significant partnership with OpenAI, integrating its AI tools like Codex into Infosys' Topaz model. This collaboration aims to revolutionize software development and modernization for enterprises, moving beyond experimentation to practical, scaled AI deployment.

articles.title: Tata Elxsi among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
articles.content: Futures open interest surged over 13% across nine NSE stocks, led by Tata Elxsi, Havells, CDSL, HCL Tech and SBI Life, signaling rising trader participation and expanding derivative positions.
articles.excerpt: Futures open interest surged over 13% across nine NSE stocks, led by Tata Elxsi, Havells, CDSL, HCL Tech and SBI Life, signaling rising trader participation and expanding derivative positions.

articles.title: SBI Life shares fall 3% after Q4 net profit declines 1%: Here's why Nomura, Nuvama, other brokerages remain bullish
articles.content: SBI Life Insurance shares dipped 3% after reporting a 1% net profit decline in Q4 FY26, despite a 16% rise in net premium income. Brokerages remain bullish, maintaining 'Buy' ratings with targets around Rs 2,400, citing stable performance and strong distribution networks. Management targets 14% APE growth and improved VNB margins.
articles.excerpt: SBI Life Insurance shares dipped 3% after reporting a 1% net profit decline in Q4 FY26, despite a 16% rise in net premium income. Brokerages remain bullish, maintaining 'Buy' ratings with targets around Rs 2,400, citing stable performance and strong distribution networks. Management targets 14% APE growth and improved VNB margins.
